ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content: [ ] (Ancient) Explore this [timeline](https://lifeofcaesar.com/timeline-of-the-life-of-gaius-julius-caesar/) of Julius Caesar's life.

[ ] (World) Listen to "[Lady Franklin's Lament - British Folk Ballad](https://youtu.be/D6Ep5B48dDA?si=7UL2cWjSoYLWnDxe)."
[ ] (World) View a [map](https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/nova/arctic/expe-nf.html) of Franklin's route.
[ ] (World) Explore the website of the [Wrecks of HMS Erebus and HMS Terror National Historic Site](https://parks.canada.ca/lhn-nhs/nu/epaveswrecks) (visit if possible!).
[ ] (Ancient) Watch [National Geographic: In the Shadow of Vesuvius](https://youtu.be/NI6C2DueJGo?si=MHqMoZElnCQ_wZ-8). \*Preview for art images
[ ] (World) Watch video: [Unification of Germany: How Prussia Reshaped Europe](https://youtu.be/NZjD_WrQAPs?si=OAIwHfh9t0kswQnS) (shows history on maps).
[ ] (Ancient) Search for women from your readings at the [Visual Museum of Women in Christianity](https://www.visualmuseum.gallery/gallery/).

resources.title: Beric the Briton: A Story Of The Roman Invasion
resources.author: G.A. Henty

resources.title: Napoleon
resources.author: David A. Bell
resources.rationale: Very readable biography of this important historical figure.
